sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 1.5g of fat. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, that is a really good sign.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 61.3g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 22.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: filling for the calories because legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety. cheap staple with real payoff because this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. fibre-forward category identity because legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre.

closing.summary: Navy Beans has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
